gaps.description: The Bill of Rights is an empty scaffold with no human-authored content. No scope of enterprise, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, or interpretation rules exist. This is the constitutional identity that the MSP must translate and Arbiter must enforce, yet it is entirely absent.

The MSP contains zero substantive rules. Nomos’s second compliance gate is effectively absent, allowing all Action Proposals to pass without any machine-actionable constraints, objectives, or safety rules.

The amendment protocol requires a PEM triggered by a blocked AP, but no MSP rules exist to block any AP, so no PEM can ever be issued. This creates a deadlock that prevents the Senate from publishing the first substantive rule through the only defined amendment path.
No genome documents exist to provide organ-specific operating rules or risk-tier-specific constraints. Organs operate without tailored constraints beyond their own code.

legacy.raw_response:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-001",
      "reference": "Bill of Rights (BoR) scaffold — Article I through VI marked [To be defined by human principal]; MSP Purpose 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives' and Arbiter reference 'Arbiter verifies the Bill of Rights at every scope query'",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is an empty scaffold with no human-authored content. No scope of enterprise, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, or interpretation rules exist. This is the constitutional identity that the MSP must translate and Arbiter must enforce, yet it is entirely absent.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "why": "Without a BoR the DIO has no legal perimeter, no defined boundaries for action, and Arbiter’s scope gate has nothing to verify. The organism operates without a constitution, making all governance downstream of it untethered from any human-intent constraint."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-002",
      "reference": "MSP Rules section — '(none)'; MSP 'The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules'",
      "description": "The MSP contains zero substantive rules. Nomos’s second compliance gate is effectively absent, allowing all Action Proposals to pass without any machine-actionable constraints, objectives, or safety rules.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "why": "Every AP is adjudicated with no operational constitution. All organs, services, and changes proceed unrestricted, undermining the entire purpose of having a rules engine. The DIO remains in default-permissive mode indefinitely."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-003",
      "reference": "MSP Amendment Protocol: 'Amendments to this document originate only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos (#200) when an AP is blocked by current MSP/genome constraints.' Combined with no MSP rules (GAP-002).",
      "description": "The amendment protocol requires a PEM triggered by a blocked AP, but no MSP rules exist to block any AP, so no PEM can ever be issued. This creates a deadlock that prevents the Senate from publishing the first substantive rule through the only defined amendment path.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "why": "The legislative process is structurally incapable of exiting the bootstrap seed state. Even if the human principal fills the BoR (GAP-001), the MSP would remain empty because the only amendment trigger—a PEM—cannot occur. The DIO is locked into default-permissive governance."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-004",
      "reference": "MSP Genome References — '(none at seed)'",
      "description": "No genome documents exist to provide organ-specific operating rules or risk-tier-specific constraints. Organs operate without tailored constraints beyond their own code.",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "low",
      "impact": "medium",
      "why": "Absence of genome rules may lead to uncoordinated behavior or missing safety nets, but this gap is secondary until the foundational BoR and MSP rules gaps are resolved. Organ-level self-protection may partially mitigate risk in the interim."
    }
  ]
}

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-OP-001",
      "description": "The MSP Rules section is empty (seed). Without ANY operational rules, the system lacks enforceable SLAs, runtime targets, organ availability thresholds, and cadence baselines. Nomos operates in default-permissive mode, meaning no action proposal is ever denied on MSP grounds—every operation is authorized regardless of risk or resource exhaustion.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP > Rules section: \"(none)\" – bootstrap seed contains no rules",
      "severity": "critical",
      "criticality": "high – system has no operational guardrails",
      "urgency": "high – every action proposal is permitted, immediate operational risk",
      "impact": "high – affects all organs, all proposals, entire DIO",
      "priority_score": 27
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-OP-002",
      "description": "No SLA for organ availability is defined. Spine-state transitions show repeated cycles of DEGRADED ↔ ALIVE for all organs in tight clusters (e.g., 17:28:07–17:28:14, all organs simultaneously degrading then recovering). Without an availability target (e.g., 99.95% per organ), there is no objective metric to trigger escalation or policy enforcement.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP > Rules section: absent SLA rules; MSP > Genome References: none defined for organ-specific operating rules",
      "severity": "high",
      "criticality": "high – degraded organs may compromise the DIO’s operational integrity",
      "urgency": "medium – currently organs recover quickly, but prolonged unseen degradation risk exists",
      "impact": "high – if multiple organs degrade simultaneously without SLA-backed response, entire cluster could fail silently",
      "priority_score": 18
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-OP-003",
      "description": "No runtime targets (latency budgets) are defined for the Nomos adjudication pipeline (Arbiter scope gate + MSP/genome compliance gate). The MSP states that an AP must pass both gates to be authorized, but there is no upper bound on how long adjudication may take, nor any penalty if it times out.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP > Purpose: describes two compliance gates but does not specify runtime SLAs",
      "severity": "high",
      "criticality": "medium – unbounded latencies can stall the entire action loop",
      "urgency": "medium – not an immediate crash risk, but could degrade user-facing response times",
      "impact": "medium – affects responsiveness of all agentic actions",
      "priority_score": 12
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-OP-004",
      "description": "Health check cadence baselines (ping interval, pong timeout, consecutive miss threshold) are not documented in MSP or genome. The Spine transitions show that all organs are marked DEGRADED simultaneously (likely a single ping sweep) and then recover within a few seconds, suggesting a ping interval that is too short or a pong timeout too aggressive, causing unnecessary flapping and state churn.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP > Rules section: absent; Genome References: none; operational cadences are a regulatory requirement per MSP’s purpose to ensure stable operations",
      "severity": "medium",
      "criticality": "medium – unnecessary flapping increases log noise and potential for false-positive incident triggers",
      "urgency": "low – no immediate failure, but chronic instability wears on operational confidence",
      "impact": "medium – state flapping can lead to spurious PEM generation and wasted Senate-Legislator effort",
      "priority_score": 6
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-OP-005",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights is a scaffold with all articles marked \"[To be defined by human principal]\". Consequently, the MSP (which is subordinate to BoR) cannot contain any constraint derived from enterprise scope, prohibited activities, core identity, or human authority reservations. The entire operational rule set is constitutionally void—MSP’s purpose cannot be fulfilled without a populated BoR.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR > Articles I–VI: all undefined; MSP > Relationship to the Bill of Rights: MSP is strictly subordinate and any conflict voids MSP rule",
      "severity": "critical",
      "criticality": "critical – no constitutional boundary exists; enterprise scope is unbounded",
      "urgency": "high – until the human principal defines the BoR, the DIO operates without a legal perimeter",
      "impact": "critical – Arbiter’s scope gate is non-functional, allowing any proposal to pass scope check regardless of BoR intent",
      "priority_score": 27
    }
  ]
}
